prerequisites: A Workday tenant with the custom reports you want to extract already built and enabled as web services. When creating each custom report, set the report type to Advanced and check 'Enable as Web service' so it is exposed at the RaaS customreport2 URL.
An Integration System User (ISU) account created in the Workday tenant with a username and password. Workday sets the ISU Session Timeout Minutes to zero so the integration session does not expire.

An Integration System Security Group (for example Integration System Security Group (Unconstrained)) linked to the ISU, granted Get access to the domain security policies covering the data the reports return, with pending security policy changes activated.
Each custom report shared with the ISU (or its security group) via the report's Share section, and the report owner's username, since the RaaS URL includes the report owner.
The Workday host for your tenant (configured as 'host', for example a *.workday.com hostname) and the tenant name (configured as 'tenant_id').
The names of the custom reports to extract (configured as 'report_ids').

authMethods.name: Basic authentication (Integration System User)
authMethods.summary: Workday RaaS custom report URLs are called over HTTP basic auth using a Workday account, typically an Integration System User. Map the ISU username into the 'credentials' object and its password into the secret field 'credentials.password'. The tenant name is supplied via 'tenant_id'. There is no separate API key or OAuth client in this connector's secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Build the custom reports and enable them as web services
setupSteps.body: In Workday, run the Create Custom Report task for each report you want to extract. Set the Report Type to Advanced and check 'Enable as Web service'. This exposes the report under the RaaS endpoint /ccx/service/customreport2/{tenant}/{owner}/{ReportName}. Note each report name to use later in 'report_ids'.

setupSteps.title: Create an Integration System User and security group
setupSteps.body: Run the Create Integration System User task to create a username and password (Workday sets Session Timeout Minutes to zero so the session does not expire). Run Create Security Group, choosing an Integration System Security Group such as Integration System Security Group (Unconstrained), and link it to the ISU.

setupSteps.title: Grant the ISU permission to the report data
setupSteps.body: Use Maintain Permissions for Security Group to give the ISU's security group Get access to the domain security policies covering the fields your reports return, then run Activate Pending Security Policy Changes so the permissions take effect.

setupSteps.title: Share each report with the ISU
setupSteps.body: Open each custom report's Share section, choose 'share with specific authorized groups and users', and add the ISU (and its security group) as authorized users/groups so the ISU can read the report over the web service.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set 'host' to your Workday host and 'tenant_id' to your tenant name (a secret value). Put the ISU username in the 'credentials' object and its password in 'credentials.password' (secret). List the custom report names to pull in 'report_ids'. Optionally set 'num_workers' to control extraction concurrency.

sources.title: Create RaaS reports for Workday (Microsoft Learn): Create Custom Report, Enable as Web service, customreport2 RaaS URL and credentials
